title: Four-Extremity Neurostimulation Using Two Cervical Octapolar Leads and High Frequency of 10 kHz
study_question: Can 10-kHz cervical SCS relieve four-extremity neuropathic pain without paresthesias?
population_assessed: 29-year-old man with C3 fracture, central cord lesion, four-extremity neuropathic pain after cervical injury.
outcome_measures: NRS pain score, functional improvement, opioid use, paresthesia presence
inclusion_criteria: Single patient with refractory four-extremity neuropathic pain after cervical injury and surgery.
answer_to_study_question: 10-kHz cervical SCS reduced pain in all extremities, improved function, and avoided paresthesias through 3 months.
reasoning: All models strongly agreed on bibliographic fields and study design. I merged consistent clinical details, favoring shared high-confidence wording for outcomes and population.
